Welcome to Magnetic Marketing Session number five, how to apply a magnetic marketing to transform your business into a money-making machine. So Dan a question that we get a lot is okay well how do I apply this online does this this work online? Well so so online really means a collection of media that's really what we're talking about right and so media is media is media and the idea that because it is online or on television or on radio or on a postcard or in the yellow pages at directory or etc etc etc that somehow now all new methodology applies is a completely false erroneous and dangerous idea. So what worked as a lead generation classified ad in the first national newspaper in America long before USA Today which was Harper's Weekly and it was published during the Civil War and there was a vibrant classified ad section full of lead generation advertising to get free reports about health and nutrition making money in real estate finding a new career surviving bullet wounds all on your own all sorts of stuff and what worked there is classified ad would work exactly as it did there posted online. PaperClick. Today right yeah paperClick is essentially buying eyeballs to look at a classified ad. That's exactly what it is. That's what it is. Box whatever. So if you just change the response instruction corn's gone in seven days or money back see free video at. You're good to go right. It's really a business philosophy. And so talk a little bit about how these really apply these fundamentals that we've talked about really applied to someone's business as a whole. Well so there's two big things. We've talked about both of them. But so one is whether you want to be in the push business the pursuit business or the attract traction business. The other is whether you want systems and marketing assets or a lot of repetitive manual labor and random and erratic acts. So if you take the second one for example and you really look at the way most businesses advertise market and sell they they're not systemized. So a lot of people read the myth. So if you no disrespect to it but if you summarize it on a three by five card it is organize the operations of your businesses. If you were going to franchise it 10,000 times across the country and staff it with kids and monkeys even if you are never going to do that. Okay. Because you will have a better business. Right. So but that's mostly and where most people do it is in the operations side. Right. So if we use our Cleveland Clinic example well unlike what I just described with what happens at and after the executive physical which from a marketing standpoint is a mess. Right. So its efficacy is totally dependent on those eight people pitching me and whether they had a fight with their wife or not before they came to work this morning and whether they like me or not and all that and there's no systemized approach to any kind of follow up. Their operations side of the Cleveland Clinic I assure you is micro detailed rigidly controlled you know here's how you do a hip replacement and if there's 412 steps to do an hip replacement by God you do them in this order and this one takes 17 minutes and it better not have taken 22 minutes or 6 minutes when we look at the film etc. So a lot of businesses really really systemize their operations and in many cases they do it in part because they discover they have no choice. Right. Because if they have employees and they send for carpet cleaners out to homes and they don't have the actual process of cleaning the carpet rigidly organized and mystery shop and supervised and all that each one of the four guys is doing it differently on different days they're screwing up they have poor customer satisfaction etc. So you see operations focused on from an organizational standpoint in a business and there's all sorts of software for it and there's you know surveillance cameras and there's you know there's an entire industry of organization of operations as systems. Right. So hence you can go pretty much to any quiz knows in America and within a small range of variants if you order sandwich X it's going to be the same it's going to take about the same number of minutes to make it's going to be cooked to the same temperature etc. They got that down hardly anybody applies the same thinking and determination to organizing their marketing and selling has systems and there's not much of an industry around doing so. Right. And there's all sorts of reasons for that. So really what magnetic marketing is all about is is to a great degree of starting to impose that kind of discipline to so financial advisors. So I had 63 of the groups. So you take a bunch of high end high income financial advisors who bring people into workshops and schedule appointments but only with a fraction of the ones who are there best ones half maybe. So if you ask them and they're all smart sophisticated and successful people you ask them okay you have 80 you book 30 what do you do with the other 50. You will get one of two answers and they both suck right and it's shocking in a way that they suck because these are smart people sophisticated people successful people. One answer you'll get is nothing happens with them. The other answer you get is I give them to Helen and Helen calls them if she has time. Well this is not a system. This is a ill-fated hope because on the list of things Helen wants to do this week calling and following up on prospects who didn't book appointments you know is right around you know foot amputation without anesthesia it's right about part. So Helen's going to find a lot of ways not to have time right. So now we need a system and it needs to be organized and disciplined and it has to happen exactly the same way and ideally there's software to support it and you know and so forth. That Q&A happens in almost every business. So if you ask the dermatologist who were here the other day so somebody comes in for a consult. And they go through the whole consultation process and you tell them now what they need to do to fix their acne or fix their scars and they don't proceed with treatment. What happens to them. You get exactly the same answer. Nothing or Helen calls them when she has time right at the big corporate level. So years ago I worked with Weight Watchers and Weight Watchers at the time fielded between a million and a million and a half inbound calls a year. And what a lot of people don't know by the way about so they don't even realize they pay for every call. So people look at what did it cost me to get a patient. What it cost me to get a lead. They don't back up all the way to what it cost me to get every call today. In many cases they don't track the data and the staff lies to them and all that stuff but you pay for every call. Whether anything happens whether or not you pay for it you pay for every like you pay for every visit. Right. You did something to cause it divided by the number you got. You paid 20 cents for them or two dollars for them or 20 dollars for them or two hundred dollars for them. So Weight Watchers a million or a million and a half calls at the time their whole system was go to meetings which is different now and not the go to meetings you go to real meetings. So all those calls were the same. That was a very modern reference. Very very impressed. Yeah. So Betty you want to lose weight. How much you want to lose. Great. Why do you want to lose it. Terrific. Then none of that mattered. Right. Great. We got meetings in your town and we have three meetings this week and here's where one two and three which one be better for you. and many businesses have at a pretty good level and a lot of businesses actually have at a great level and doing the same thing to your marketing so you wind up with ownership of systems instead of random and erratic acts you and others have to do that never get done the same way twice and often only get done under duress oh crap we owe quarterly taxes and we don't have much money in the bank we in pursuit do you want to be in attraction how do you want your customers to feel about you do you want to feel like they had product pushed on them do you want to feel like they were pursued uh uh tackled drug into the cave and have their wallet taken out of their pocket or do you want to have them feel like they were attracted to you and came to you and created relationship with you so you can talk to say ben glass in our world who provides marketing coaching that really grew from magnetic marketing to lawyers but also still operates a very successful law practice right what ben will tell you is that magnetic marketing brings to him a pre-qualified predetermined prospective client who is really there to hire him if they can right as opposed to a skeptical anxious nervous perhaps fee resistant difficult client who must be convinced from the very ground zero to completion to hire him and that's fatiguing right so like in my life nothing is more fatiguing for me and I avoid it almost with perfection as having a new client come to spend a day with me who is not already sold on me and on my approach to marketing right because I'm going to be a tired puppy at the end of the day if I had to do 10 of those days in a row I wouldn't be here because I would be dead right I would have put the gun in the mouth long ago and you know checked out so that really just just takes the heart out of people right in many of their businesses and that's totally unnecessary this can replace it you know the other thing that it does is it puts you in control that's that is was the biggest days out right at best if you're making it for her birthday or your wedding anniversary maybe you do it a week a week and a half okay this allows people it allows them to sell that package six months in advance so it even changes the cash flow economics of the business it allows them to deliver a different message than everybody else is delivering that's giving them differentiation tied to the unique positioning it creates some price elasticity because it's not about now what's the average price of a dinner at your restaurant see it's not about that it allows us to have some fun which the letter the stuff is written in fun and there's a difference between being in fun and being funny funny is a much harder hurdle and a much more dangerous one in advertising and marketing because you could be so funny that everybody laughs a lot but doesn't remember the entity the thing being advertised 30 seconds later and you will see a lot of that during the Super Bowl too so we could wait five
